samp_mat_process: Water samples were collected by CTD rosette from the Canadian Coast Guard ship M. Perley in the offshore zone of Nova Scotia. Triplicate one-litre water samples were collected at each sample site  and filtered onto a 1.2um PES filter using a Smith-Root vacuum pump in the lab. A field blank consisting of distilled water was also filtered at the same time to check for potential contamination in the field.
geo_loc_name: Canada: Nova Scotia, St. Anns Bank
LClabel: We acknowledge that this work has taken place in Mi’kma’ki, the ancestral and unceded territory of the Mi’kmaq People, who are part of the Wabanaki (Dawnland Confederacy). Our relationship to this place is one of a visiting scientist, learning from its waters. Our commitment is to move beyond words by ensuring scientific findings are shared publicly and accompanied by this acknowledgement to inform both science and a respectful relationship to the Mi’kmaq communities who have stewarded these lands and waters since time immemorial. We acknowledge their enduring sovereignty, knowledge, and contributions, past, present, and future.

samp_mat_process: Water samples were collected from a small boat approximately 0.5 metres below the surface using a Smith-Root sampling pole and vacuum pump. Samples were collected from freshwater, brackish, and marine habitats in this estuary.
LClabel: We acknowledge that this work has taken place on the unceded, ancestral territory of the Passamaquoddy, Wolastoqiyik (Maliseet), and Wabanaki (Dawnland Confederacy) peoples. Our commitment to this place is rooted in the act of acknowledgement—of these nations, their languages of Passamaquoddy, Maliseet-Passamaquoddy, and Mikmaq, and the enduring Peace and Friendship Treaties. In the spirit of these treaties, our stewardship is an action: to ensure the data from these waters is shared publicly, accompanied by this acknowledgement, so that care for this marine protected area is informed by both science and a respectful relationship to the Indigenous lands and waters that sustain us all.
